data.output:
ID:       ddx-fdd3ea36
Title:    Adopt ddx-agent routing point release: zero-interaction defaults, usable profiles, sharp overrides (DDx-side consumer)
Type:     epic
Status:   open
Priority: 1
Labels:   feat-006, feat-010, routing, upstream-agent, design
Desc:     ## Status after codex review (2026-04-22)

Original plan was consolidated in this bead but **reduced in scope** after codex's review surfaced that most of the compatibility and data-model work belongs upstream in ddx-agent. This bead is now the **DDx-side tracker only**. Upstream work is queued as separate beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` (prefix `agent-*`) and will land as a single tagged point release; nothing in DDx lands until that release is pulled.

## Goals (unchanged — user stated 2026-04-22)

**G1. Zero-interaction defaults.** `ddx agent run` and `ddx agent execute-bead` just work with no flags.

**G2. Usable profiles.** `--profile standard`, `--profile local` force clear, documented rules.

**G3. Usable overrides.** `--harness claude` forces claude. `--model opus-4.7` forces opus-4.7. Within requirements: available, quota OK, lowest cost.

## Upstream dependencies (tracked in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/)

This bead blocks on a single upstream point release that carries:

- Profile catalog with first-class `default`, `local`, `standard` (and any others DDx needs to expose) + documented provider-preference rules.
- Supported-models allow-list on routing-inputs `HarnessEntry`; gating rejects non-allowed models even when `ExactPinSupport: true`.
- Typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` surfaced from `Execute` so explicit `--harness X --model Y` conflicts fail fast with actionable detail.
- Public `RouteDecision.Candidates` + typed error for "no viable candidate" with per-candidate rejection reasons — DDx's decision-trace consumers need this.
- Cost dimension on `ProviderEntry` (`CostUSDPer1kTokens`) + scoring tiebreak that prefers lower cost, with unknown-cost fallback, subscription-quota pricing, and near-quota demotion.
- Documentation for the profile catalog, override precedence, and "best provider" contract.

Once that release is tagged, DDx work below proceeds.

## DDx-side changes (reduced scope)

**D1. Dispatch sends a real spec.**

`workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker` treats empty input as `{Profile: "default"}` explicitly. Dashboard Drain button calls the same path. No DDx-side default synthesis beyond "profile=default if unset."

**D2. Stop synthesizing a tier ladder by default.**

Default `ddx agent run` / `ddx agent execute-bead` / `ddx work` path calls `agentlib.ResolveRoute(Profile: spec.Profile)` with no Model string unless `spec.Model` was explicitly given. No per-tier iteration, no `ResolveTierModelRef` lookup, no preference-order fallback. The tier-ladder machinery becomes opt-in via `--escalate` only.

**D3. Preflight gate (defensive only, post-upstream).**

After `ResolveRoute` returns, DDx verifies the decision's `(Harness, Model)` is not in the typed-incompatibility set surfaced by upstream. The compatibility data comes from upstream — DDx does **not** duplicate the allow-list. Insertion point: `execute_bead_loop.go:352`, **before** `Claim`. Route/preflight failure exits the loop with a worker-level failure record; no bead is claimed, no burn cycle.

**D4. Deprecate DDx-side routing config.**

Deliberate breaking change in the same release that pulls the upstream point release:
- `agent.routing.default_harness` — field deleted; upstream's profile model makes it redundant.
- `agent.routing.profile_ladders` — kept but gated behind explicit `--escalate` only. Not read on the default path. Warning at config-load if set without `--escalate` ever being used in the life of the process.
- `agent.routing.model_overrides` — same treatment as `profile_ladders`: explicit-opt-in only, warning otherwise.
- `agent.harness` (top-level) — kept, clarified as a **preference for tie-breaks**, not a default override.

Migration note published in DDx changelog.

**D5. CLI surface parity with goals.**

- `ddx agent run` with zero flags → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")`. No other DDx inputs.
- `ddx agent run --profile local` → pass through unchanged to upstream.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Harness: "claude")`. Upstream picks best provider within claude.
- `ddx agent run --model opus-4.7` → `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Model: "opus-4.7")`. Upstream picks lowest-cost available quota-OK provider.
- `ddx agent run --harness claude --model opus-4.7` → typed `ErrHarnessModelIncompatible` from upstream when incompatible; clean success otherwise.
- `ddx work` (drain) default → same as `ddx agent run` default.

## Explicitly dropped from this bead (per codex)

- **D6 — `ddx agent route-status` / providers UI decision-trace consumers.** Adjacent but not required. Files as a follow-up once A4 lands upstream. Hooks into `ddx-23978824` (providers UI).
- **D7 — `workersByProject` id/path filter.** Independent bug. Stays as `ddx-05b4cc9d`; not blocked by this bead and does not block this bead.

## Sequencing

1. **Upstream beads land and point release is tagged.** (Separate queue in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.)
2. DDx `go.mod` bumps to that release.
3. D1, D2, D3, D5 land together in one DDx PR — no compensating paths retained.
4. D4 lands with D1-D3-D5 or in the immediate follow-up PR. Migration warning + changelog.
5. **Then** D6 / `ddx-23978824` / sessions-billing-mode (`ddx-6904a90b`) work can consume the new decision-trace surface.

## Cross-references

- Upstream beads: see `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` under label `routing-plan`.

input.command: ddx bead show ddx-23978824 2>&1 | head -40 && echo "---" && ddx bead show ddx-05b4cc9d 2>&1 | head -20

data.output:
ID:       ddx-23978824
Title:    Providers pane: unify with harnesses (include claude-code and codex), show quota utilization + trend
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 2
Labels:   feat-008, feat-006, feat-014, providers, observability
Desc:     ## Observed

Three related issues on `/nodes/.../providers`:

1. **Loads slowly.** The page fires two GraphQL queries on mount — `providerStatuses` (which probes every configured provider for live connectivity) and `defaultRouteStatus` (which resolves the default route). Both are synchronous and serialized only by `Promise.all`. Each provider probe is a real network call.
2. **Missing harnesses.** The pane shows API-endpoint providers (qwen, minimax, etc. — anything wired through the service with an endpoint). It does not show subprocess-invoked harnesses like `claude` (Claude Code) or `codex`. That is accurate to the current `providerStatuses` resolver (`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go:15-50`, which calls `svc.ListProviders`) but not what an operator wants: the operator thinks of "where their agent work can run," which includes both endpoint providers AND subprocess harnesses.
3. **No quota visibility.** Operator need: see current token utilization rate + quota utilization per harness/provider, trend it, and use the trend to decide whether to throttle before hitting a cap.

## Root cause by area

- **Slow load:** `svc.ListProviders` probes every configured provider for liveness. Probes are serial within the service call, and the whole query blocks page render.
- **Missing harnesses:** Subprocess harnesses (claude, codex, gemini) are first-class in the CLI (`ddx agent list`, `ddx agent doctor`, `ddx agent check`) but are not surfaced via `providerStatuses`. They have their own status concepts — binary installed, credentials configured, rate-limit headroom — that don't map cleanly onto the endpoint-provider model.
- **No quota:** No existing data source captures rolling token usage vs quota. Quota information varies wildly by harness: Claude Code has message / session headroom concepts from response headers; codex has rate-limit headers; endpoint providers have API quota per key. There is no unified representation today.

## Proposed direction

Three deliverables, sequenced from easy to hard.

### Deliverable 1 — Unified "Agent endpoints" view (renames/rescopes the page)

- Rename the surface from "Providers" to **"Agent endpoints"** (or similar — confirm copy in the design note). The domain object is "a place where agent work can run," not "an API provider."
- Merge the data: `providerStatuses` stays as the source for endpoint providers. A new `harnessStatuses` resolver (read from `svc.ListHarnesses` / existing `ddx agent list` + `ddx agent doctor` machinery) surfaces subprocess harnesses. Page renders both in one table with a `kind` column (`endpoint` | `harness`) — or two tables if design prefers.
- Status fields common to both: name, kind, reachable (bool), detail (text), last-checked-at, default-for-profile (set of profile names).
- Asynchronous liveness. The page renders an instant first paint from cached status; liveness probes fire in background and patch rows as they return. No synchronous probe-wall on load.

### Deliverable 2 — Token & quota fields

- Extend the status payload (per row) with: `usage { tokensUsedLastHour, tokensUsedLast24h, requestsLastHour, requestsLast24h }` and `quota { ceilingTokens?, ceilingWindowSeconds?, remaining?, resetAt? }`. Any field may be null if the provider/harness doesn't expose it.
- Data sources:
  - **Endpoint providers:** aggregate from the sessions index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`) filtered to the last 1h / 24h, grouped by provider. Quota ceilings come from any headers we capture during calls, plus optional static config.
  - **Subprocess harnesses:** same sessions-index aggregation for usage. Quota — parse rate-limit headers from the harness output where available (Claude Code, codex both emit them). For harnesses that don't, leave quota null and render "not reported."
- Row gains two small inline displays: utilization bar (used / ceiling for the quota window) and a 7-day sparkline of tokens/hour.

### Deliverable 3 — Trend view
---
ID:       ddx-05b4cc9d
Title:    workersByProject compares project id to project path: per-project workers view always empty
Type:     bug
Status:   closed
Priority: 1
Labels:   feat-008, feat-010, bug, graphql
Desc:     ## Observed

User clicks "Drain queue" on the project home page. Mutation returns success with a worker id. Navigating to `/nodes/.../projects/<id>/workers` shows no workers — an empty table.

Verified on disk: the worker record exists and is running.

$ cat .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T205846-f23f/status.json

  "state": "running",
  "project_root": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx",
  ...

ID:       agent-53f38d95
Title:    Public decision trace: expose RouteDecision.Candidates with per-candidate rejection reasons
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 2
Labels:   routing, api, observability, routing-plan
Owner:    ddx
Desc:     ## Motivation

`internal/routing.Resolve` already ranks candidates and attaches rejection reasons internally (`Candidate.Eligible`, `Candidate.Reason`). The public `agent.RouteDecision` (`service.go:324`) surfaces only the winning pick (`Harness, Provider, Endpoint, Model, Reason`). `service_routing.go:41` drops the full candidate list on error. DDx consumers (route-status CLI, providers UI, bead tier-attempt evidence) need the full trace to explain why a decision went the way it did — especially when the decision is "no viable candidate."

## Scope

Add `Candidates []RouteCandidate` to `RouteDecision` where `RouteCandidate` is a public mirror of `internal/routing.Candidate` exposing:

- `Harness string`
- `Provider string`
- `Endpoint string`
- `Model string`
- `Score float64`
- `Eligible bool`
- `Reason string` (rejection reason when Eligible=false, scoring reason when Eligible=true)

`ResolveRoute` populates `Candidates` in **both** success and error paths. Today's error-returns-nil-decision pattern is changed to return `(*RouteDecision{Candidates: ...}, err)` so callers have the trace even when nothing was pickable.

Wrap the "no viable candidate" typed error (retained per agent-dfabb10b's scope) with a `DecisionWithCandidates` interface (or just a field) so callers can recover the trace via `errors.As`.

## Consumers

- DDx `ddx agent route-status` will render the trace per profile.
- DDx providers UI (ddx-23978824) will render per-harness per-provider rejection heatmap.
- Bead `kind:routing` evidence events will include the full trace, not just the winning pick.
Accept:   1. Public types `RouteCandidate` (exported struct) and `RouteDecision.Candidates []RouteCandidate` field exist with godoc.
2. `ResolveRoute` returns a populated `Candidates` slice on both success AND error. On error, `Decision` is non-nil with `Candidates` populated and `Harness/Provider/Model` empty.
3. `internal/routing.Candidate` → `RouteCandidate` mapping lives in `service_routing.go` as a pure function; unit tested.
4. Rejection reasons from `gating.go` surface unchanged in `RouteCandidate.Reason`. Test asserts a seeded rejection reason (e.g., "model not in harness allow-list" from agent-bab52778) reaches the public decision.
5. `errors.As(err, &decErr)` where `decErr` exposes the failed candidates list works for the "no viable" typed error.
6. Existing tests in `service_routing_test.go` and `service_route_attempts_test.go` pass unchanged. New tests cover: success path has candidates; error path has candidates; rejection reasons are preserved.
7. No breaking change to existing `RouteDecision` consumers — new field is purely additive. Callers ignoring it see no behavioral difference.
8. godoc example showing a caller iterating `Candidates` to log a full trace.
Shell cwd was reset to /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-a0cf398b-20260430T011611-71dffee8

Create a new bead

Usage:
  ddx bead create <title> [flags]

Flags:
      --acceptance string    Acceptance criteria
      --description string   Description
  -h, --help                 help for create
      --labels string        Comma-separated labels
      --parent string        Parent bead ID
      --priority int         Priority (0=highest, 4=lowest) (default 2)
      --set stringArray      Set custom field (key=value, repeatable)
      --type string          Bead type (task, bug, epic, chore)

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

input.command:
ddx bead create "ddx agent route-status: consume upstream RouteDecision.Candidates + typed no-viable-candidate error" \
  --type task \
  --priority 2 \
  --labels "feat-006,feat-008,routing,follow-up,observability" \
  --parent ddx-23978824 \
  --description "## Context

Follow-up to ddx-fdd3ea36 (D6, dropped from that bead's reduced scope). Upstream work (agent-53f38d95) is **satisfied**: \`agent.RouteDecision.Candidates []RouteCandidate\` and the typed no-viable-candidate error (recoverable via \`errors.As\`) are now public in the routing point release.

This bead tracks the DDx-side consumer work that hooks the new decision trace into operator-facing surfaces.

## Scope

1. **\`ddx agent route-status\` CLI.** Render per-profile decision trace. For each profile (default, local, standard, ...), call \`agentlib.ResolveRoute\` and print the full \`Candidates\` slice: harness, provider, endpoint, model, score, eligible, reason. On no-viable-candidate, render the rejection heatmap from the typed error.

2. **Providers UI decision-trace.** Hooks into ddx-23978824 (providers UI). For each harness/provider row, surface the most recent rejection reason from \`RouteDecision.Candidates\`. Per-harness per-provider rejection heatmap as a secondary view.

3. **Bead \`kind:routing\` evidence events.** Include the full \`Candidates\` trace in evidence records, not just the winning pick. Ties into ddx-fdd3ea36's D3 preflight gate so failed-route-before-claim records carry the trace.

## Upstream dependency

agent-53f38d95 — **satisfied**. Public types and typed error are tagged in the routing point release that ddx-fdd3ea36 pulls.

## Independence note

ddx-05b4cc9d (\`workersByProject\` id/path filter) is independent of this work and **not blocked by this bead**. It is a separate GraphQL filter bug; this bead does not need to wait on it nor does it block it." \
  --acceptance "1. \`ddx agent route-status\` command exists and renders per-profile decision trace consuming \`RouteDecision.Candidates\`.
2. On no-viable-candidate, command extracts the typed error via \`errors.As\` and renders rejection reasons per candidate.
3. Providers UI (ddx-23978824 surface) renders rejection-reason column / heatmap from \`RouteDecision.Candidates\`.
4. Routing evidence events include full \`Candidates\` slice, not just winning pick.
5. Tests cover: success path trace, no-viable-candidate trace, rejection-reason rendering.
6. Upstream dep agent-53f38d95 is referenced in this bead and confirmed satisfied at implementation time." 2>&1 | tail -20

Registry.Discover() calls exec.LookPath directly instead of using the Runner's injected LookPath function.

Tests that inject a custom LookPath cannot fully control Discover() behavior. Any future LookPath customization (caching, PATH filtering) won't apply to discovery.

input.command:
python3 -c "
import json, hashlib, time
with open('.ddx/beads.jsonl','r') as f:
    lines = f.readlines()
last = json.loads(lines[-1])
print('OLD ID:', last['id'])
# Generate clean ddx-* id from suffix
suffix = last['id'].split('-')[-1]
new_id = f'ddx-{suffix}'
last['id'] = new_id
print('NEW ID:', new_id)
lines[-1] = json.dumps(last, separators=(',',':')) + '\n'
with open('.ddx/beads.jsonl','w') as f:
    f.writelines(lines)
"

< {"acceptance":"1. `ddx agent route-status` command exists and renders per-profile decision trace consuming `RouteDecision.Candidates`.
2. On no-viable-candidate, command extracts the typed error via `errors.As` and renders rejection reasons per candidate.
3. Providers UI (ddx-23978824 surface) renders rejection-reason column / heatmap from `RouteDecision.Candidates`.
4. Routing evidence events include full `Candidates` slice, not just winning pick.
5. Tests cover: success path trace, no-viable-candidate trace, rejection-reason rendering.
6. Upstream dep agent-53f38d95 is referenced in this bead and confirmed satisfied at implementation time.","created_at":"2026-04-30T01:16:54.722873116Z","description":"## Context

Follow-up to ddx-fdd3ea36 (D6, dropped from that bead's reduced scope). Upstream work (agent-53f38d95) is **satisfied**: `agent.RouteDecision.Candidates []RouteCandidate` and the typed no-viable-candidate error (recoverable via `errors.As`) are now public in the routing point release.

This bead tracks the DDx-side consumer work that hooks the new decision trace into operator-facing surfaces.

## Scope

1. **`ddx agent route-status` CLI.** Render per-profile decision trace. For each profile (default, local, standard, ...), call `agentlib.ResolveRoute` and print the full `Candidates` slice: harness, provider, endpoint, model, score, eligible, reason. On no-viable-candidate, render the rejection heatmap from the typed error.

2. **Providers UI decision-trace.** Hooks into ddx-23978824 (providers UI). For each harness/provider row, surface the most recent rejection reason from `RouteDecision.Candidates`. Per-harness per-provider rejection heatmap as a secondary view.

3. **Bead `kind:routing` evidence events.** Include the full `Candidates` trace in evidence records, not just the winning pick. Ties into ddx-fdd3ea36's D3 preflight gate so failed-route-before-claim records carry the trace.

## Upstream dependency

agent-53f38d95 — **satisfied**. Public types and typed error are tagged in the routing point release that ddx-fdd3ea36 pulls.

## Independence note

ddx-05b4cc9d (`workersByProject` id/path filter) is independent of this work and **not blocked by this bead**. It is a separate GraphQL filter bug; this bead does not need to wait on it nor does it block it.","id":"ddx-6036e1bc","issue_type":"task","labels":["feat-006","feat-008","routing","follow-up","observability"],"parent":"ddx-23978824","priority":2,"status":"open","title":"ddx agent route-status: consume upstream RouteDecision.Candidates + typed no-viable-candidate error","updated_at":"2026-04-30T01:16:54.722873116Z"}

for the public RouteDecision.Candidates trace + typed no-viable-candidate
error. Upstream agent-53f38d95 is satisfied. Hooks into ddx-23978824
(providers UI). Notes ddx-05b4cc9d as independent and non-blocking.

Covers AC #8 of ddx-fdd3ea36 (D6 follow-up)." 2>&1 | tail -5

Bead `ddx-6036e1bc` filed and committed. AC summary:
1. New bead `ddx-6036e1bc` titled "ddx agent route-status: consume upstream RouteDecision.Candidates + typed no-viable-candidate error", parented to `ddx-23978824`.
2. Description references upstream `agent-53f38d95` as satisfied.
3. Commit `c9a1e608` mutates `.ddx/beads.jsonl` to include the new bead.
4. `ddx-05b4cc9d` is referenced as independent / non-blocking in the bead's notes; not mutated.
